title: Public opinion shift in Sweden regarding climate action during the rise of the Fridays for Future movement

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on historical SOM Institute trends prior to movement peak
Estimated: Interpolated growth following initial COP24 momentum
Estimated: Surge following March 15 global climate strike
Estimated: Sustained media coverage in Swedish press
Estimated: Alignment with 2019 Pew Research findings

events.description: Greta Thunberg begins her school strike for climate outside the Swedish Parliament
Greta Thunberg addresses the COP24 UN Climate Change Conference in Poland
Global Climate Strike sees millions of participants worldwide

agents.name: Greta Thunberg
agents.description: Primary activist and initiator of the school strike movement

agents.name: Swedish Green Party
agents.description: Political party advocating for aggressive climate policy

agents.name: Swedish Industrial Lobby
agents.description: Represents manufacturing and energy interests concerned about rapid transition costs

agents.name: Fridays for Future Youth
agents.description: Core group of student activists organizing local strikes

agents.name: Rural Conservative Voters
agents.description: Demographic concerned about fuel prices and urban-centric policy

agents.name: Swedish Media Outlets
agents.description: Mainstream press providing significant coverage of the movement

notes: Pro-side is defined as individuals who support urgent government action to mitigate climate change. Against-side is defined as those who oppose such measures or prioritize economic stability over climate policy. Data quality is high for the endpoint (Pew Research), while intermediate rounds are estimates based on the trajectory of media coverage and movement growth.
